what_it_does: Implements a React widget that lets users bring external material into a project via file upload, image upload, browser screen recording, and connector-based services; stores each captured item in a project context store and exposes a per-source toggle for whether Vi will read it.
observations: Uses React hooks aliased as useStateSI/useRefSI/useEffectSI, then reassigns Text/TOK from window.MS_ATOMS.
Renders four affordance buttons from an array: upload files, add images, screen recording, and connect a service.
Uses hidden file inputs for generic and image uploads and infers source kind from MIME type.
Screen recording uses navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia plus MediaRecorder and handles the user stopping the share from browser chrome.
Connector chooser lists window.MS_CONNECTORS and opens a ConnectorWizard for the selected provider.
Captured sources list shows thumbnails for images/videos, glyphs for other kinds, and provides read/skip toggles and remove buttons.
The component self-registers with window.MS_REGISTRY, declaring its schema, emitted events, tags, and metadata.
Relies on globals: window.MS_CONTEXT, window.MS_ATOMS, window.MS_CONNECTORS, window.MS_REGISTRY, plus undefined-in-file helpers Icon, Chip, ConnectorWizard, and connectorById.
The compact prop is accepted but not used anywhere in the layout logic.
standouts: Real browser screen-recording integration via getDisplayMedia and MediaRecorder.
Self-registering component pattern that carries its own schema, emits, and registry metadata.
Per-source willRead toggle giving explicit user control over AI ingestion.
Heavy reliance on window.* global singletons rather than imports.
conventions: Array-driven UI rendering for affordances and connectors.
useRef for hidden inputs and media recorder state.
concerns: Screen-recording blob is hardcoded to video/webm regardless of the actual recorder MIME type.
Object URLs created for images/videos are not revoked when sources are removed, risking memory leaks.
connectorById, Icon, Chip, and ConnectorWizard are used but not defined in this file.
No validation or limits for file size, count, or duplicate names.
The compact prop is declared but unused.
Recording error state is only set, never cleared automatically after a successful retry.
notes: This is the project's 'bring in material' intake surface, wiring real browser APIs and connector wizards into a shared context store.
questions: Where are Icon, Chip, ConnectorWizard, and connectorById defined?
What is the exact shape and behavior of window.MS_CONTEXT.addSource, setSourceRead, and removeSource?
How is the compact prop intended to change the layout?
Are file size, count, or duplicate-name limits enforced elsewhere?
What does window.MS_REGISTRY do with the registered metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header comment describes upload/image/record/connector intake into project context, and the code implements exactly those affordances.
apparent_themes: Context-first AI assistance: all user material is centralized so Vi can read it.
Self-describing components: widgets carry their own schema, emits, and registry metadata.
Progressive disclosure: the connector chooser appears only when requested.
intention_signals: Build a low-friction, all-in-one ingestion surface that treats files, images, screen recordings, and cloud connectors as interchangeable source material for an AI assistant.
novelty: Combines native file pickers, live screen recording via getDisplayMedia, and a connector mini-wizard in a single self-registering widget with per-source AI-read toggles.
contribution: A reusable intake widget that captures heterogeneous source material and records it in project context, with explicit user control over which items the AI reads.
summary_for_embedding: A self-registering React source-intake widget that uploads files and images, records the screen via getDisplayMedia/MediaRecorder, connects cloud services through a connector wizard, and stores captured sources in a project context store with per-item read/skip toggles.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single surface for feeding files, images, screen recordings, and connector data into an AI assistant's project context.

symbols.does: Renders the source-intake UI and manages upload, recording, and connector state.
symbols.description: Main functional React component that displays a grid of intake affordances, hidden file inputs, a connector chooser, a screen-recording flow using getDisplayMedia, and a list of captured sources with read/skip toggles; it also self-registers with the global component registry.

symbols.does: Accepts a FileList and stores each file as a project source.
symbols.description: Maps browser File objects into context sources, inferring kind from MIME type and creating object URLs for images and videos; then optionally notifies the parent via onEmit.

symbols.does: Captures the screen and audio using getDisplayMedia and records with MediaRecorder.
symbols.description: Requests display media, wires MediaRecorder data into chunks, stops cleanly when the user ends the share, and saves the resulting webm blob as a project video source.

symbols.does: Stops the active MediaRecorder if one is running.
symbols.description: Simple control function that halts the in-progress screen recording so the onstop handler can finalize the captured video.
